question_paper_html:
Annual Exam - 2020

Code: 56/4/2 (set-2)

  1. Please check that this question paper contains 15 printed pages.
  2. Code number given on the right hand side of the question paper should be written on the title page of the answer-book by the candidate.
  3. Please check that this question paper contains 37 questions.
  4. Please write down the Serial Number of the question in the answer-book before attempting it.
  5. 15 minute time has been allotted to read this question paper. The question paper will be distributed at 10.15 a.m. From 10.15 a.m. to 10.30 a.m., the students will read the question paper only and will not write any answer on the answer-book during this period.

CHEMISTRY (Theory)

Time allowed:3 hours
Maximum Marks:70

General Instructions :

Read the following instructions very carefully and strictly follow them:

  1. Question paper comprises four sections - A, B, C and D.
  2. There are 37 questions in the question paper. All questions are compulsory.
  3. Section - A : Q. No. 1 to 20 are very short answer type questions carrying one mark each. Answer these questions in one word or one sentence.
  4. Section - B : Q. No. 21 to 27 are short answer type questions carrying two marks each.
  5. Section - C : Q. No. 28 to 34 are long answer type-I questions carrying three marks each.
  6. Section - D : Q. No. 35 to 37 are long answer type-II questions carrying five marks each.
  7. There is NO overall choice in the question paper. However, an internal choice has been provided in 2 questions of two marks, 2 questions of three marks and all the 3 questions of five marks. You have to attempt only one of the choices in such questions.
  8. However, separate instructions are given with each section and question, wherever necessary.
  9. Use of calculators and log tables is NOT permitted.

SECTION – A

Read the given passage and answer the questions 1 to 5 that follow:

Colloidal particles always carry an electric charge which may be either positive or negative. For example, when AgNO3 solution is added to KI solution, a negatively charged colloidal sol is obtained. The presence of equal and similar charges on colloidal particles provide stability to the colloidal sol and if, somehow, charge is removed, coagulation of sol occurs. Lyophobic sols are readily coagulated as compare to lyophilic sols.

  1. What is the reason for the charge on sol particles?
  2. Why the presence of equal and similar charges on colloidal particles provide stability?
  3. Why a negatively charged sol is obtained on adding AgNO3 solution to KI solution?
  4. Name one method by which coagulation of lyophobic sol can be carried out.
  5. Out of KI or K2SO4, which electrolyte is better in the coagulation of positive sol?

Questions 6 to 10 are one word answers:
6.

Name the depressant which is used to separate PbS and ZnS containing ore in froth floatation process.
7.

Out of  and  which will react faster in SN1 reaction with OH-?
8.

Out of CH3NH2 and CH3OH, which has higher boiling point?
9.

Which one of the following is a narcotic analgesic?
Penicillin, Codeine, Ranitidine
10.

Write the name of linkage joining two monosaccharides.

Questions 11 to 15 are Multiple Choice Questions:
11.

The coordination number of Co in the complex [Co(en)3]3+ is:

a. 3
b. 6
c. 4
d. 5
12.

An electrochemical cell behaves like an electrolytic cell when

a. Ecell = Eexternal
b. Ecell =0
c. Eexternal > Ecell
d. Eexternal < Ecell
13.

The half-life period for a zero order reaction is equal to
(where [R]0 is initial concentration of reactant and k is rate constant.)

a. {tex}\frac{0.693}{k}{/tex}
b. {tex}\frac{2k}{[R]_0}{/tex}
c. {tex}\frac{2.303}{k}{/tex}
d. {tex}\frac{[\mathrm{R}]_0}{2 \mathrm{k}}{/tex}
14.

The crystal field splitting energy for octahedral ({tex}\Delta_o{/tex}) and tetrahedral ({tex}\Delta_t{/tex}) complexes is related as

a. {tex}\Delta_t =\frac{2}{9} \Delta_{ o }{/tex}
b. {tex}\Delta_t=\frac{5}{9} \Delta_o {/tex}
c. {tex}\Delta t =\frac{4}{9} \Delta_o{/tex}
d. {tex}\Delta_t=2 \Delta_o{/tex}
15.

{tex}\alpha{/tex}–D(+) glucose and {tex}\beta{/tex}–D(+) glucose are

a. Geometrical isomers
b. Enantiomers
c. Anomers
d. Optical isomers

Questions 16 to 20:

  1. Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are correct statements, and Reason (R) is the correct explanation of the Assertion (A).
  2. Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are correct statements, but Reason (R) is not the correct explanation of the Assertion (A).
  3. Assertion (A) is correct, but Reason (R) is wrong statement.
  4. Assertion (A) is wrong, but Reason (R) is correct statement.
16.

Assertion (A): F2 has lower bond dissociation enthalpy than Cl2..
Reason (R): Fluorine is more electronegative than chlorine.

a. Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are correct statements, and Reason (R) is the correct explanation of the Assertion (A).
b. Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are correct statements, but Reason (R) is not the correct explanation of the Assertion (A).
c. Assertion (A) is correct, but Reason (R) is wrong statement.
d. Assertion (A) is wrong, but Reason (R) is correct statement.
17.

Assertion (A): Hydrolysis of an ester follows first-order kinetics.
Reason (R): Concentration of water remains nearly constant during the course of the reaction.

a. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
b. Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
c. A is true but R is false.
d. A is false but R is true.
18.

Assertion (A): Transition metals have high melting point.
Reason (R): Transition metals have completely filled d-orbitals.

a. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
b. Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
c. A is true but R is false.
d. A is false but R is true.
19.

Assertion (A): (CH3)3 C–O–CH3 gives (CH3)3 C–I and CH3OH on treatment with HI.
Reason (R): The reaction occurs by SN1 mechanism.

a. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
b. Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
c. A is true but R is false.
d. A is false but R is true.
20.

Assertion (A): Benzoic acid does not undergo Friedel-Crafts reaction.
Reason (R): The carboxyl group is activating and undergo electrophilic substitution reaction.

a. Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
b. Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.
c. A is true but R is false.
d. A is false but R is true.

SECTION–B

Write the balanced chemical equations involved in the preparation of KMnO4 from pyrolusite ore (MnO2).

OR

Write the balanced ionic equations showing the oxidising action of acidified dichromate ({tex}Cr_2O_7^{2-}{/tex}) solution with

What happens when

  2. acetone is added to pure ethanol?

23.

Write IUPAC name and hybridization of the following complexes:

  1. [Ni(CO)4],
  2. [CoF6]3- (Atomic number Ni = 28, Co = 27)
 2
24.

State Henry’s law. Calculate the solubility of CO2 in water at 298 K under 760 mm Hg. (KH for CO2 in water at 298 K is 1.25 {tex}\times{/tex} 106 mm Hg)

 2
25.

Define the following terms with a suitable example in each:

  1. Tranquilizers
  2. Anionic detergent
 2
26.

Write the reactions showing the presence of following in the open structure of glucose:

  1. an aldehyde group
  2. a primary alcohol
 2
27.

Write the principle of the following refining methods:

  1. vapour phase refining
  2. chromatography
 2

OR

Write chemical equations involved to obtain :

  1. Cu from Cu2S
  2. Ag from [Ag (CN)2]– complex

SECTION : C
28.

The rate constant for the first order decomposition of N2O5 is given by the following equation:
k = (2.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 1014 s-1) e(-25000K)/T
Calculate Ea for this reaction and rate constant if its half-life period be 300 minutes.

 3
29.

Following ions are given:
Cr2+, Cu2+, Cu+, Fe2+, Fe3+, Mn3+
Identify the ion which is

  1. a strong reducing agent.
  2. unstable in aqueous solution.
  3. a strong oxiding agent.

Give suitable reason in each.

 3

The freezing point of a solution containing 5g of benzoic acid (M = 122 g mol-1) in 35g of benzene is depressed by 2.94 K. What is the percentage association of benzoic acid if it forms a dimer in solution? (Kf for benzene = 4.9 K kg mol-1)

31.

Write the name and structures of monomers in the following polymers:

  2. Terylene

    (CH3)3N, (CH3)2NH, CH3NH2

    (C2H5)2NH, (C2H5)3N, C2H5NH2

Write the product(s) of the following reactions:

  3. Identify (A) and (B) in the following:

How can you convert the following?

SECTION : D
35.
  1. Account for the following:
    1. Tendency to show –2 oxidation state decreases from oxygen to tellurium.
    2. Acidic character increases from HF to HI.
    3.  Moist SO2 gas acts as a reducing agent.
  2. Draw the structure of an oxoacid of sulphur containing S–O–S linkage.
  3. Complete the following equation:
    XeF2 + H2O {tex}\to{/tex} 
 5

OR
  1. Among the hydrides of group 16, write the hydride
    1. Which is a strong reducing agent.
    2. Which has maximum bond angle.
    3. Which is most thermally stable.
      Give suitable reason in each.
  2. Complete the following equations:
    {tex}S + \mathop {{H_2}S{O_4}}\limits_{(Conc.)} \to{/tex}
    {tex}C{l_2} + \mathop {NaOH}\limits_{(cold\,\,and\,\,dilute)} \to {/tex}
 5
36.
  1. The electrical resistance of a column of 0.05 M KOH solution of length 50 cm and area of cross-section 0.625 cm2 is 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 103 ohm. Calculate its resistivity, conductivity and molar conductivity.
  2. Predict the products of electrolysis of an aqueous solution of CuCl2 with platinum electrodes.
    (Given: {tex}\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{Cu}^{2+} / \mathrm{Cu}}^{\mathrm{o}}{/tex} = +0.34 V, {tex}E^o _{(1/2 Cl_2 Cl^-)}{/tex} = +1.36 V
    {tex}\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{H}^{+} / \mathrm{H}_{2}(\mathrm{g}), \mathrm{Pt}}^{\mathrm{o}}{/tex} = 0.00 V, {tex}E^o _{(1/2 O_2/H_2O)}{/tex} = +1.23 V)
 5

OR
  1. Calculate e.m.f. of the following cell:
    Zn(s)/Zn2+ (0.1 M) || (0.01 M) Ag+/Ag(s)
    Given : {tex}\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{Zn}^{2+} / \mathrm{Zn}}^{\circ}{/tex} = –0.76 V, {tex}\mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{Ag}^{+} / \mathrm{Ag}}^{\circ}{/tex} = + 0.80 V
    [Given : log 10 = 1]
  2. X and Y are two electrolytes. On dilution molar conductivity of ‘X’ increases 2.5 times while that Y increases 25 times. Which of the two is a weak electrolyte and why?
 5
37.
  1. An organic compound (A) having molecular formula C4H8O gives orange red precipitate with 2, 4-DNP reagent. It does not reduce Tollens’ reagent but gives yellow precipitate of iodoform on heating with NaOH and I2. Compound (A) on reduction with NaBH4 gives compound (B) which undergoes dehydration reaction on heating with conc. H2SO4 to form compound (C). Compound (C) on Ozonolysis gives two molecules of ethanal.
    Identify (A), (B) and (C) and write their structures. Write the reactions of compound (A) with (i) NaOH/I2 and (ii) NaBH4.
  2. Give reasons:
    1. Oxidation of propanal is easier than propanone.
    2. {tex}\alpha{/tex}-hydrogen of aldehydes and ketones is acidic in nature.
 5

OR
  1. Draw structures of the following derivatives:
    1. Cyanohydrin of cyclobutanone
    2. Hemiacetal of ethanal
  2. Write the major product(s) in the following:
    1. {tex}{\mathrm{CH}}_3-\mathrm{CH}=\mathrm{CH}-{\mathrm{CH}}_2-\mathrm{CN}\xrightarrow[{(\mathrm{ii})\;{\mathrm H}_3\mathrm O^+}]{(\mathrm i)\;\mathrm{DIBAL}-\mathrm H}{/tex}
    2. {tex}{\mathrm{CH}}_3-{\mathrm{CH}}_2-\mathrm{OH}\xrightarrow{{\mathrm{CrO}}_3}{/tex}
  3. How can you distinguish between propanal and propanone?
 5

760 = 1.25 {tex}\times{/tex} 106 {tex}\times{/tex} x

x = 6.08 {tex}\times {/tex} 10-4

This technique was first used for the separation of colours and hence, the name. There are two phases in chromatography - the stationary phase and the mobile phase. There are many different types of chromatography - paper chromatography, thin-layer chromatography, gas chromatography, etc.

  1. An organic compound (A) having molecular formula C4H8O gives orange red precipitate with 2, 4-DNP reagent. It does not reduce Tollens’ reagent but gives yellow precipitate of iodoform on heating with NaOH and I2. Compound (A) on reduction with NaBH4 gives compound (B) which undergoes dehydration reaction on heating with conc. H2SO4 to form compound (C). Compound (C) on Ozonolysis gives two molecules of ethanal.
    Identify (A), (B) and (C) and write their structures. Write the reactions of compound (A) with (i) NaOH/I2 and (ii) NaBH4.
  2. Give reasons:
    1. Oxidation of propanal is easier than propanone.
    2. {tex}\alpha{/tex}-hydrogen of aldehydes and ketones is acidic in nature.
Solution:
  1. The molecular formula of compound (A) is C4H8O, which suggests that it may be an aldehyde or a ketone. The positive reaction with 2,4-DNP also supports this idea. The negative Tollens' test indicates that it is not an aldehyde. The positive reaction with NaOH and I2 suggests that it has a methyl ketone group (CH3CO-).
    Therefore, compound (A) is 2-butanone (methyl ethyl ketone) with the structure CH3COCH2CH3.
    When 2-butanone (A) is reduced with NaBH4, it forms the corresponding alcohol, which is compound (B). The reaction is:
    CH3COCH2CH3 + NaBH4 {tex}\to{/tex} CH3CH(OH)CH2CH3
    Compound (B) is 3-pentanol with the structure CH3CH(OH)CH2CH3.
    When 3-pentanol (B) is dehydrated with conc. H2SO4, it forms compound (c), which is an alkene. The reaction is:
    CH3CH(OH)CH2CH3 {tex}\to{/tex} CH3CH=CHCH3 + H2O
    When 2-pentene (C) is subjected to ozonolysis, it forms two molecules of ethanal. The reaction is:
    CH3CH=CHCH3 + O3 {tex}\to{/tex} CH3CHO + CH3CHO
    Therefore, the structures of compounds (A), (B), and (C) are:
    (A) 2-butanone (methyl ethyl ketone): CH3COCH2CH3
    (B) 3-pentanol: CH3CH(OH)CH2CH3
    (C) 2-pentene: CH3CH=CHCH3
    Reactions of compound (A):
    (i) Reaction with NaOH/I2:
